public_context: NaphCare is a private correctional-healthcare provider awarded a five-year, ~$1.4B ($279.7M/yr) full-risk Arizona DOC inmate medical/mental/dental contract effective Oct 2022. In Feb 2026 a federal judge ordered a court-appointed receivership over Arizona prison healthcare with authority to renegotiate or terminate the NaphCare contract after sustained constitutional-violation litigation.

Pulice-FNF-Flatiron is a recognizable joint venture of three heavy-civil highway contractors (Pulice Construction, FNF Construction, Flatiron) that builds major Arizona freeway projects under ADOT, typically Maricopa Association of Governments freeway-program and federal-aid-funded design-build contracts (e.g., the Loop 202 South Mountain Freeway corridor).

Connect 202 Partners is the Fluor-led LLC (with Granite Construction, Ames Construction and Parsons Brinckerhoff) awarded ADOT's ~$1.66B Loop 202 South Mountain Freeway design-build-maintain contract - Arizona's largest highway project and first freeway P3, built 2016-2019 with a 30-year maintenance term.

Kiewit-Fann is a joint venture of Kiewit Infrastructure and Fann Contracting (both established Arizona/Southwest civil contractors), formed to deliver ADOT's Interstate 17 Anthem-to-Sunset Point widening — the state's largest current freeway project, procured as a design-build-maintain contract with a financed/debt-service component.

Security Title Agency, Inc. is one of Fidelity National Financial's largest Arizona title brands (50+ years in the state) providing title insurance and escrow/closing services; it has a dedicated escrow manager for ADOT government/right-of-way projects, matching the highway-acquisition disbursements seen in the data.

primary_agency: DEPT OF EMERGENCY AND MILITARY AFFAIRS
agencies: DEPT OF EMERGENCY AND MILITARY AFFAIRS
public_context: AshBritt Inc, a Florida disaster-response contractor, built Governor Doug Ducey 2022 Arizona shipping-container border wall under a no-bid sole-source emergency contract (AshBritt was the only company considered), paid about 194.7M to erect and later remove the barrier; total cost exceeded 200M plus about 76M for teardown after federal objections, drawing oversight and procurement scrutiny.

public_context: Vizient Inc is a large U.S. healthcare performance-improvement/group-purchasing company (Irving, TX). In July 2020 it partnered with the Arizona Dept of Health Services to bring ~600 out-of-state nurses into overwhelmed hospitals during the COVID-19 surge; the staffing/labor-management arm was later acquired by Aya Healthcare.

Public Consulting Group (PCG) is a private management-consulting firm that AHCCCS contracts as third-party administrator of Arizona's Medicaid School-Based Claiming Program (Direct Service + Medicaid Administrative Claiming); the most recent contract runs July 2022-June 2025 and PCG has served in this role for 11+ years.

Coffman Ames Joint Venture (involving Ames Construction, a major national heavy-civil contractor) was ADOT's design-build prime contractor for the approximately $185M Loop 101 (Pima Freeway) widening project from I-17 to Pima Road, which began in February 2019. Ames is also part of the Connect 202 Partners JV on the Loop 202 South Mountain Freeway.

GBC Properties LLC appears to be an Arizona real-estate/land-holding entity; the payments align with ADOT right-of-way land acquisition for the Maricopa Regional Area Road Fund (RARF) freeway program rather than a service/construction contract.

Fisher Sand & Gravel Co. (also Fisher Industries) is a large, long-established North Dakota-based heavy-civil construction and aggregates contractor active in Arizona highway work; it is a well-known DOT roadbuilding contractor (and gained national attention for border-wall bids). Its ADOT freeway/grading payments are ordinary public-works contracting.

Granite Construction Co (NYSE: GVA) is a large publicly-traded U.S. heavy-civil/highway contractor with a Tucson, AZ office and a long-documented ADOT relationship, including ~$170M I-10 Tucson reconstruction, I-10 pavement rehab, the $55M Grant Road widening, and the Loop 202 P3. Its presence in ADOT CAPITAL OUTLAY is exactly what would be expected.

Zeitlin & Zeitlin, P.C. is a Phoenix, AZ law firm (founded 1993, Dale & Dawn Zeitlin) specializing in eminent domain and inverse condemnation, representing property owners in government takings; principal Dale Zeitlin has handled over $100M in eminent-domain cases. ADOT payments to it are right-of-way acquisition/condemnation just-compensation payouts.

primary_agency: EARLY CHILDHOOD DEVELOP AND HEALTH BOARD
agencies: EARLY CHILDHOOD DEVELOP AND HEALTH BOARD
public_context: Local Initiatives Support Corporation (LISC) is a major national nonprofit community-development financial intermediary. Its Phoenix office administered Arizona's ~$60M Child Care Infrastructure Grant program (federal ARPA/CCDF pandemic relief) on behalf of DES and First Things First, awarding facility-improvement grants to 478 child care providers statewide.

transactions.agency: EARLY CHILDHOOD DEVELOP AND HEALTH BOARD
EARLY CHILDHOOD DEVELOP AND HEALTH BOARD
EARLY CHILDHOOD DEVELOP AND HEALTH BOARD
EARLY CHILDHOOD DEVELOP AND HEALTH BOARD

public_context: Driggs Title Agency Inc is an Arizona-based title and escrow company; such firms act as the neutral closing/escrow agent through which a government buyer disburses funds when acquiring real property, including highway right-of-way parcels.

CGI Technologies and Solutions Inc. is the US arm of CGI Inc., a major publicly traded Canadian IT/systems-integration firm; it is the long-standing integrator behind Arizona's statewide AFIS (Arizona Financial Information System) ERP and the HRIS replacement.

Arizona State University is a public research university; its Biodesign Institute operated Arizona's statewide COVID-19 saliva-based testing program under contract with the AZ Dept of Health Services during the pandemic.

The Weitz Company was the construction-manager-at-risk (with architect SmithGroup) for the Arizona Department of Veterans' Services on the Flagstaff and Yuma State Veteran Homes — two 74,000 sq ft, 80-bed facilities; groundbreaking 2019, scheduled completion June 2021. This confirms the FY2021 draws as routine progress payments on a documented, publicly announced capital project.

Coffman Specialties, Inc. is an established heavy-civil/highway construction firm (founded 1991, Woman-Owned Business Enterprise) with documented major ADOT design-build contracts, including a ~$410M I-10 corridor project (with Fisher Sand & Gravel/Stantec) and a $41.9M US-60/Bell Road interchange in Surprise, AZ.

Deloitte Consulting LLP is one of the Big Four professional-services firms and a leading government IT systems integrator; it commonly holds multi-year state contracts for replacing legacy child-support, eligibility, and enterprise application systems.

"Estrella" is a master-planned community in Goodyear, Arizona (southwest Maricopa County), in the SR-30 / Loop 303 freeway corridor; "CRE-WPL Estrella JV LLC" reads as a commercial-real-estate joint venture entity. A targeted web search confirmed only the general ADOT right-of-way land-acquisition context, not the specific JV (single-purpose land-holding LLCs are not independently searchable).

Sundt Construction is a major employee-owned general contractor headquartered in Tempe, Arizona (founded 1890), and one of the largest builders in the Southwest, with a long history of Arizona Department of Transportation highway and freeway work.

DonorsChoose is a well-known national education nonprofit. The Arizona Department of Education publicly partnered with it using federal ESSER COVID-relief funds (announced $14M and an additional $5M efforts) to let public-school teachers request classroom resources (~$1,000/project cap), funding 15,000+ teacher projects.

Payee name is redacted in the data (the literal string 'NAME REDACTED' is a placeholder token shared by ~1,821 distinct vendor IDs), so no specific named entity can be identified or searched. The Arizona AG's Consumer Restitution and Remediation Revolving Fund is the statutory vehicle (A.R.S. consumer-fraud statutes) for disbursing settlement proceeds, frequently to counties and claims administrators.

GCOM Software LLC (now rebranded Voyatek) was selected by the Arizona Dept of Revenue in Aug 2023 for a ~15-year, ~$79.1M contract to build the STARS integrated tax system (RevHub platform, with subcontractor Kyndryl). The multi-year modernization is publicly documented and ongoing (completion ~2029-2030).

entity_name: EMERY SAPP & SONS INC AND SUBSIDIARIES

public_context: ACTIVE RESOURCE MANAGEMENT, LLC (ARM) was a water-asset holding company (owned by Vidler Water Company) that sold 50,000 Long Term Storage Credits to the Arizona Water Banking Authority for $12.5M (~$250/credit). Local reporting (Desert Water Grab) characterized it as a private sale to the water bank at a notably high price relative to ARM's underlying acquisition cost.

primary_agency: DEPT OF EMERGENCY AND MILITARY AFFAIRS

public_context: Sundt Construction is a large employee-owned, Arizona-headquartered general/heavy-civil contractor (founded 1890); 'CS A JOINT VENTURE' denotes a project-specific JV it forms for ADOT freeway/transportation work.

Centurion of Arizona LLC (a Centene/MHM subsidiary) was awarded the Arizona Department of Corrections statewide inmate correctional-healthcare contract (RFP ADOC18-00008264), effective July 1, 2019, at roughly $204M per year to serve the state's 10 prison facilities.

public_context: Pitney Bowes Inc. (NYSE: PBI) is a publicly traded global shipping, mailing, and postage-meter company; state agencies routinely prefund Pitney Bowes postage meters in round lump sums, which matches every pattern in this data.

public_context: Fondomonte Arizona LLC is the US subsidiary of Saudi dairy giant Almarai, growing alfalfa on Arizona state land to ship home as cattle feed; its cheap leases and unmetered groundwater pumping drew national scrutiny, an Arizona Auditor General investigation, and lease terminations/non-renewals by Gov. Hobbs in 2023. Public reporting indicates the $7M was a state-law-required reimbursement to Fondomonte for lease improvements.

The Arizona Jewish Historical Society received a $7M FY2025 state appropriation (via the Dept of Education) to help build the Hilton Family Holocaust Education Center in Phoenix, which broke ground in 2025 and is slated to open in 2027 with ~$38M total raised; a condition was free admission for Arizona students.

Motorola Solutions Inc. (NYSE: MSI, headquartered near Chicago) is the dominant U.S. provider of public-safety land-mobile-radio (P25) systems; multi-year, multi-million-dollar statewide radio contracts with state police/DOT agencies are its core line of business.

public_context: GEO Secure Services LLC is a subsidiary of The GEO Group (NYSE: GEO), a publicly traded private-prison and detention operator that runs Arizona facilities including ASP-Kingman, Florence West, and Phoenix West under long-standing Arizona Department of Corrections contracts.

public_context: Kiewit Development Company was a short-listed proposer team in ADOT's Loop 202 South Mountain Freeway design-build P3 procurement; it lost to Connect 202 Partners (Fluor/Granite/Ames), which won the ~$916M contract finalized in 2016. The $2M is the standard losing-proposer stipend paid to defray proposal-development costs.

Pulice Construction Inc is a long-established Arizona heavy-highway/freeway construction contractor (Phoenix area) that is a frequent ADOT prime/design-build contractor on Loop and freeway projects. ADOT routinely pays fixed 'stipends' to design-build proposers, which are round lump-sum amounts.
